title: Digital Consent Artifact Cannot Be Verified
scenario: A digital consent artifact exists, but its signature, timestamp, or provenance cannot be verified before execution.
initial_state: A digital consent artifact was stored in the clinical record.
state_transition: Verification of the artifact fails during execution evaluation.
execution_boundary: The clinical action is evaluated before relying on the digital consent artifact.
constitutional_evaluation: Consent artifact existence does not establish consent continuity unless the artifact remains verification-eligible.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because the digital consent artifact cannot support reconstructable consent continuity.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must fail closed when consent evidence exists but cannot be verified.
clinical_action: Execute procedure based on unverifiable digital consent
